marketPulseSubtext: Oil up, yields up, equities down hard
topDriverSubtext: Inflation fear back on the tape
disclaimer: Prototype disclaimer: This screen reflects a manual March 26, 2026 market snapshot, but it is not validated for trading, investment, or risk decisions. It would need to be hooked up to real-time market and news sources, then scored by tested algorithms and monitoring before production use.
rateCurveRead: As of the March 26 snapshot, Treasury yields climbed sharply again as oil surged and markets pared back any remaining hopes for rate cuts.

crossMarket.driver: Growth hit by yields and war risk

crossMarket.trend: Worst drop since Iran war began

drivers.name: Iran rejection / war escalation
drivers.detail: Markets sold off hard after Iran rejected a U.S. ceasefire proposal, reviving fears of a prolonged conflict and worsening supply disruption.

drivers.name: Oil shock returns
drivers.detail: Brent surged above $108 and WTI above $94 as Hormuz disruption fears intensified, putting inflation anxiety back at the center of the tape.

drivers.name: Rate-cut hopes collapse
drivers.detail: Treasury yields jumped as investors priced out any meaningful chance of near-term cuts and even revived talk of further hikes.

drivers.name: Tech-led valuation compression
drivers.detail: The Nasdaq led the decline as higher yields and geopolitical stress hit long-duration growth assets hardest.

risks.title: Extended oil shock
risks.desc: A prolonged Hormuz disruption could keep energy prices rising and deepen inflation stress.

risks.title: Further equity de-rating
risks.desc: Higher yields and falling cut expectations can keep pressuring equity valuations, especially in growth.

risks.title: Cross-asset volatility spike
risks.desc: War headlines and weak bond-auction demand are driving abrupt moves across oil, bonds, and stocks.

risks.title: False de-escalation headlines
risks.desc: Any brief peace optimism can reverse quickly, creating whipsaw conditions across all major asset classes.

timeline.headline: Iran rejected the U.S. ceasefire proposal, reviving fears of a longer war.
timeline.effect: Oil rose sharply and global risk sentiment deteriorated.

timeline.headline: U.S. stocks opened lower as crude moved back above $100.
timeline.effect: The S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq all sold off immediately.

timeline.headline: Treasury yields climbed further after another weak government-debt auction.
timeline.effect: Financial conditions tightened and growth stocks underperformed.

timeline.headline: Wall Street closed with its worst drop since the Iran war began.
timeline.effect: The Nasdaq entered correction territory and risk-off sentiment dominated.

strategies.title: Stay Defensive
strategies.body: With oil surging and yields rising, defensive positioning remains favored until markets show a real de-escalation signal.
strategies.trigger: Oil stays elevated and yields keep rising

strategies.title: Fade Growth Rallies
strategies.body: Growth-heavy equities remain vulnerable while the macro backdrop tightens financial conditions and pushes valuations lower.
strategies.trigger: Nasdaq bounces while yields and crude stay firm

strategies.title: Respect Energy Leadership
strategies.body: Energy can continue to outperform as long as conflict risk and supply concerns remain unresolved.
strategies.trigger: Crude holds near or above current highs

strategies.title: Watch for Headline Reversal
strategies.body: A credible de-escalation headline could trigger a violent reversal lower in oil and higher in beaten-down risk assets.
